task_completion.description: The source image is opened in an image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editor (like GIMP) is open and displaying the source image. Note: The image may have a tan/yellow background despite the task description mentioning a white background.
A selection tool (e.g., Fuzzy Select / Magic Wand) is used, indicated by a dashed outline ('marching ants') separating the background from the main subject.

task_completion.description: The background is removed to create transparency.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The background area is deleted or masked out, revealing a checkerboard pattern behind the subject, which indicates transparency.

task_completion.description: The export dialog is configured with the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export' or 'Save As' dialog is open, with the file name set to 'output.png' and the destination folder set to '/home/ga/Pictures'.

task_completion.description: The transparent PNG is successfully exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The export dialog is confirmed/closed, a PNG export settings prompt is accepted, or the new 'output.png' file is visible in the file manager under /home/ga/Pictures.

integrity.description: The agent used the image editor's GUI to remove the background.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the image editor's tools (selection, delete, layers) rather than using terminal commands (like ImageMagick) to process the image.

integrity.description: The agent edited the provided source image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being edited should be the original source image provided in the environment (which has a tan/yellow background), not a different image downloaded from the internet.

integrity.description: The agent exported the image using the editor's GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use the 'File -> Export As' menu in the editor, rather than running a script or terminal command to convert or move the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: CRITICAL: The task description asks to remove a 'white' background, but the actual source image provided by the environment has a tan/yellow background. Do not penalize the agent for removing a tan/yellow background instead of a white one. The source image is a JPEG, so the agent must add an alpha channel or layer mask to achieve transparency (visually indicated by a checkerboard pattern). The final evaluation requires >=70% of the background to be transparent and >=85% of the subject to remain opaque.
